description: Ambawalage Sujeewa Priyadarshani Silva Vs. Debt Conciliation Board and Others Ambawalage Sujeewa Priyadarshani Silva Vs. Debt Conciliation Board and Others View More before Hon. Dr. D. F. H. Gunawardhana, J.
parties: Ambawalage Sujeewa Priyadarshani Silva Vs. Debt Conciliation Board and Others Ambawalage Sujeewa Priyadarshani Silva Vs. Debt Conciliation Board and Others View More
judgement_by: Hon. Dr. D. F. H. Gunawardhana, J.
keywords: The Petitioner made an application to the Debt Conciliation Board to have a debt settled on the basis that the Deed of Transfer on the face of it was only executed to secure a mortgage, which the Board declined after inquiry. The Petitioner invoked the jurisdiction of this Court challenging the same. Held – Since the Petitioner never parted with the possession of the land in suit after signing of the impugned deed, never intended to transfer the beneficial interest, and the huge disparity between the value of the property in suit and the consideration passed, writs issued as prayed for as the Board had erred in facts and in law.
legistation: The Constitution of the Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka

parties: Nekath Gedara Tikiri Banda and Another Vs.Sri Lanka Mahaweli Authority and Others Nekath Gedara Tikiri Banda and Another Vs.Sri Lanka Mahaweli Authority and Others View More
judgement_by: Hon. Dr. D. F. H. Gunawardhana, J.
keywords: The two Petitioners are the eldest children of one grantee who had nominated another child (the 7th Respondent) under a permit issued in respect of the same land in suit prior to the grant. No nomination was made under the grant. Later, having intended to cancel the original nomination, is said to have effected the cancellation and made a new nomination which had not been communicated to the relevant authorities to accept and register the same. Held – original nomination survives; Application of the Petitioners seeking to quash the decision to accept the 7th Respondent as the successor to the rights of the grantee is dismissed.
legistation: The Constitution of the Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka

description: S Potencia L.L.C-FZ Petitioner Vs. Lanka Coal Company (Pvt) Ltd and Others S Potencia L.L.C-FZ Petitioner Vs. Lanka Coal Company (Pvt) Ltd and Others View More before Hon.Dr. D. F. H. Gunawardhana, J.
parties: S Potencia L.L.C-FZ Petitioner Vs. Lanka Coal Company (Pvt) Ltd and Others S Potencia L.L.C-FZ Petitioner Vs. Lanka Coal Company (Pvt) Ltd and Others View More
judgement_by: Hon.Dr. D. F. H. Gunawardhana, J.
keywords: The Petitioner is a foreign company who had entered into a contract with the 1st Respondent (Government-owned company) for the purpose of supplying coal to the Norochcholai Power Plant. The main agreement contained an arbitral agreement; however, despite the arbitral agreement, the Petitioner sought to invoke the writ jurisdiction of this Court to quash the cancellation of the contract entered into between the parties. Held – Court does not have jurisdiction when an arbitral clause exists as per Section 5 of the Arbitration Act, No. 11 of 1995; Application dismissed without cost.
legistation: The Constitution of the Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka

description: Jayawardhana Rathnayake Arachige Gamini Darmashri Vs. The Divisional Secretary of Valachchenai and Others Jayawardhana Rathnayake Arachige Gamini Darmashri Vs. The Divisional Secretary of Valachchenai and Others View More before Hon. Dr. D. F. H. Gunawardhana, J.
parties: Jayawardhana Rathnayake Arachige Gamini Darmashri Vs. The Divisional Secretary of Valachchenai and Others Jayawardhana Rathnayake Arachige Gamini Darmashri Vs. The Divisional Secretary of Valachchenai and Others View More
judgement_by: Hon. Dr. D. F. H. Gunawardhana, J.
keywords: The Petitioner is a lawful lessee under a lease entered into between the Petitioner and the General Manager of Railways. The 2nd Respondent (the Additional Divisional Secretary of Valachchenai), purporting to be acting under the State Lands (Recovery of Possession) Act and having sent a quit notice on the basis that the land belongs to the State, instituted an action in the Magistrate’s Court against the Petitioner to eject him from the land in suit. Held – the 2nd Respondent cannot send quit notices because he is not the competent authority as far as Railway Lands are concerned; writ lies to quash the quit notices as well as the Magistrate Court proceedings since the Petitioner is a lessee under the Railway Department.
legistation: The Constitution of the Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka

description: Dodampahalage Primal Gerard Fernando Vs. The Employee\'s Trust Fund Board and Others Dodampahalage Primal Gerard Fernando Vs. The Employee\'s Trust Fund Board and Others View More before Hon. Dr. D. F. H. Gunawardhana, J.
parties: Dodampahalage Primal Gerard Fernando Vs. The Employee\'s Trust Fund Board and Others Dodampahalage Primal Gerard Fernando Vs. The Employee\'s Trust Fund Board and Others View More
judgement_by: Hon. Dr. D. F. H. Gunawardhana, J.
keywords: The Petitioner was attached to the 1st Respondent company as the general manager; the 4th to 10th Respondents, who are said to be leaders of a certain trade union, led a mob, and barged into the Petitioner’s office chamber and demanded his resignation by threatening by death, subject to wrongful restraint and unlawful confinement. The Police had taken actions based on a complaint, but the 1st to 3rd Respondents never took any disciplinary actions against the 4th to 10th Respondents; therefore, the Petitioner sought the intervention of this Court. Held – Writ of Mandamus issued compelling the 1st to 3rd Respondents to take the necessary disciplinary actions against the 4th to 10th Respondents.
legistation: The Constitution of the Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka

description: Dr. Rathnamali Sovis Vs. Members of the Presidential Commission of Inquiry and Others Dr. Rathnamali Sovis Vs. Members of the Presidential Commission of Inquiry and Others View More before Hon. Dr. D. F. H. Gunawardhana, J.
parties: Dr. Rathnamali Sovis Vs. Members of the Presidential Commission of Inquiry and Others Dr. Rathnamali Sovis Vs. Members of the Presidential Commission of Inquiry and Others View More
judgement_by: Hon. Dr. D. F. H. Gunawardhana, J.
keywords: The Petitioner’s husband was the former Senior DSG who assisted and advised the Anti-Corruption Committee set up by the Yahapalana Government between 2015 to 2019. The President of the successive Government of 2020 appointed a Commission of Inquiry in terms of Act No. 17 of 1948 to inquire into allegations of political victimisation during the Yahapalana regime; on a complaint made by a person who had been indicted before the High Court on the findings of the Anti-Corruption Committee where the Petitioner’s husband had acted as the advisor on the secondment and later was the prosecutor before the High Court, the Commission inquired into the allegations and made certain recommendations without his participation. Held – the Mandate did not permit the Commissioners to look into the said matter, and they had flouted the rules of natural justice; Petitioner’s husband had acted according to law and within the parameters as a lawyer attached to the Attorney General’s Department; writs issued.
legistation: The Constitution of the Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka

description: Don Duminda Roshan Mayadunna Vs. Chief Inspector of Police of Kollupitiya and Others Don Duminda Roshan Mayadunna Vs. Chief Inspector of Police of Kollupitiya and Others View More before Hon. Dr. D. F. H. Gunawardhana, J.
parties: Don Duminda Roshan Mayadunna Vs. Chief Inspector of Police of Kollupitiya and Others Don Duminda Roshan Mayadunna Vs. Chief Inspector of Police of Kollupitiya and Others View More
judgement_by: Hon. Dr. D. F. H. Gunawardhana, J.
keywords: The Petitioner claimed that he came into possess an immovable property under a sale agreement pending the final transfer deeds; however, the 6th Respondent challenged that position because the vendor to the sales agreement did not have title to the land in suit. When the 6th Respondent lost possession, he made a complaint to the police and filed a private plaint in the Magistrate’s Court under Section 66 of the Primary Court Procedure Act; both proceedings were challenged by the Petitioner. Held – Application dismissed because the Petitioner could have invoked alternative remedies and the Magistrate’s Court acted within the law in both proceedings.
legistation: The Constitution of the Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka

judgement_by: Hon. Dr. D. F. H. Gunawardhana, J.
keywords: The Petitioners challenge the publication of the collective agreement entered into between the employer and the representatives of its employees, under the supervision of the 1st to 3 rd Respondents, on the basis that they have interest in the matter and the said agreement is not beneficial to the employees since it is against the Regulations made under the Wages Board Ordinance. Held – Application dismissed with cost since the agreement is consistent with the law.
